statement.headerDoc:
STEP Hall — Hall conjecture empirical small-gap witnesses
  ==========================================================

  Hall Conjecture (1971, OPEN 55 years):
    For every ε > 0, there exists C_ε such that for all positive integers x, y
    with x² ≠ y³,  |x² − y³| > C_ε · x^(1/2 − ε).

  Implied by the ABC conjecture (Elkies, 1991). Best known Hall triples
  (small |x² − y³| relative to x^{1/2}) come from classical searches
  (Danilov, Bremner-Cassels).

  Rei empirical (scripts/hall-conjecture-verify-rei-lens.ts, x ≤ 10^5):
    Top-1 quality: (x=1138, y=109) with |x²-y³| = 15, quality ≈ 2

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Zero-sorry verified by lake env lean
